title: scenario_bec
description: An attacker compromises a senior manager's email account and uses it to redirect a large customer payment to a fraudulent bank account.

timeline.title: Manager Account Compromised
timeline.description: An attacker silently accesses a senior manager's email account via phishing and monitors conversations for weeks.

timeline.title: Payment Interception Planned
timeline.description: Attacker identifies an upcoming large payment and prepares to redirect it by impersonating the manager.

timeline.title: Fraudulent Bank Details Sent
timeline.description: An email from the manager's real account instructs finance to update the supplier's bank details before processing payment.

timeline.title: Payment Initiated
timeline.description: Finance processes the payment to the new account. Funds leave the company's account.

timeline.title: Fraud Discovered
timeline.description: The real supplier contacts accounts to follow up on an overdue payment, revealing the fraud.

timeline.title: Bank Alert and Recovery Attempt
timeline.description: The bank is contacted urgently to attempt to freeze or recall the payment before it is withdrawn.

node-1.title: Unusual Bank Detail Change Request
node-1.situation: A finance team member receives an email from the senior manager's real email address, asking them to update a long-standing supplier's bank account details before processing a scheduled payment of £95,000 next week. The email says the supplier called requesting the change.
node-1.question: What should the finance team member do?

options.label: A
options.text: Follow the bank detail change procedure: call the supplier directly using a contact number from the original contract (not from the email) to verify the change.

options.label: B
options.text: Update the bank details and process the payment — the email came from the manager's real account.

options.label: C
options.text: Reply to the manager's email to confirm the request before updating the details.

node-2.title: Supplier Denies Requesting Change
node-2.situation: The supplier confirms they never requested any bank detail change. This indicates the manager's email account has been compromised and an attacker is monitoring conversations.
node-2.question: What immediate steps should be taken?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately report to IT security to investigate and lock the manager's email account, and do not process any payments until the account is cleared.

options.label: B
options.text: Process the payment using the original bank details on file and then inform IT.

node-3.title: Mailbox Compromise Investigation
node-3.situation: IT security finds the manager's account was accessed from an overseas IP 3 weeks ago via a phishing link. The attacker set up an inbox rule to auto-forward all emails to an external address, hiding all activity.
node-3.question: What remediation steps should IT security take on the compromised mailbox?

options.label: A
options.text: Remove all malicious inbox rules, revoke all active sessions, reset credentials, enable MFA, and audit the mailbox for other suspicious activity.

options.label: B
options.text: Reset the password only — removing the forwarding rule is sufficient.

node-4.title: Finance Process Improvement
node-4.situation: The fraud was nearly successful because there was no enforced dual-approval or out-of-band verification process for bank detail changes. The finance process relied solely on email confirmation.
node-4.question: What control should be introduced to the payment change process?

options.label: A
options.text: Require dual approval from two finance team members plus direct phone verification to the supplier using a number from original records for any bank detail change.

options.label: B
options.text: Add an email confirmation step where the requester must reply to confirm — email alone is sufficient.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: No fraudulent payment was made. The compromised account has been fully remediated. Dual-approval and out-of-band verification are now mandatory for all bank detail change requests. The incident has been reported to the relevant financial crime authority.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
